steps.title: Verify RST is a right triangle and locate the right angle
steps.content:
Check whether the Pythagorean theorem holds. Testing if the sides satisfy $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$:

$$504^2 + 128^2 = 254016 + 16384 = 270400$$
$$520^2 = 270400$$

Since $RS^2 + ST^2 = TR^2$, triangle RST is a right triangle with the right angle at S (where the two legs meet).

steps.title: Use similarity correspondence to locate angle W
steps.content:
Given that triangle RST is similar to triangle UVW with correspondences $S \leftrightarrow V$ and $T \leftrightarrow W$:

Since the right angle is at S in triangle RST, it must be at V in triangle UVW. This means angle W in the similar triangle corresponds to angle T in the original triangle.

Therefore: $\angle W = \angle T$

steps.title: Calculate tan W using angle T in the right triangle
steps.content:
In right triangle RST with the right angle at S, angle T has:
  • Opposite leg: $RS = 504$
  • Adjacent leg: $ST = 128$

$$\tan T = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{504}{128}$$

Simplify by dividing both numerator and denominator by their GCD of 8:
$$\tan T = \frac{504 \div 8}{128 \div 8} = \frac{63}{16}$$

Since $\angle W = \angle T$, we have $\tan W = \frac{63}{16}$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
